name: Taka Hallah (Make 2 Days Before Shabbas)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, sugar, salt, saffron, and yeast.
2. Add oil all at once; mix gradually with water.
3. Beat for 2 minutes at medium speed of the mixer.
4. Add eggs, 1/2 cup flour, and food coloring. Beat at high speed for 2 more minutes.
5. Stir in the remaining 6 cups flour. Place dough and sides of bowl lightly with a little oil.
6. Cover with a moist towel and refrigerate for at least 24 hours.
The next day:
7. Punch down the dough and let it rise again in the refrigerator for 12 to 24 hours.
The following day:
8. Turn out the dough onto a floured board; divide into 4 pieces.
9. Press raisins into each section of dough using a kneading motion.
10. Divide each loaf into 4 pieces (total of 16 pieces). Roll each piece about 1 inch thick and about 8 to 10 inches long.
11. Pinch three pieces together at one end and braid; pinch the other end.
12. Place the fourth piece on top of the braid and pinch the ends.
13. Let stand, covered with a dry towel, at room temperature for 1 hour.
14. Brush lightly with slightly beaten egg and sprinkle with poppy seeds.
15. Bake at 400°F for 20 to 25 minutes. Makes four loaves.
